intended_use: MRI Planner is a software-only medical device intended for use by trained radiation oncologists, dosimetrists and physicists to process images from MRI systems to 1) provide the operator with informat
intended_use_full: MRI Planner is a software-only medical device intended for use by trained radiation oncologists, dosimetrists and physicists to process images from MRI systems to 1) provide the operator with information of tissue properties for radiation attenuation estimation purposes in photon external beam radiotherapy treatment planning, and to 2) derive contours for input to radiation treatment planning by assisting in localization and definition of healthy anatomical structures.
target_condition: primary and metastatic cancers in the brain and head-neck regions, as well as soft tissue cancers in the pelvic region
ground_truth: For dose accuracy testing: conventional CT images. For auto-contouring testing: manual expert delineations based on US clinical guidelines generated by two expert raters using consensus approach.
other_metrics: Dose accuracy - mean target dose difference: 0.02% ± 0.31% (pelvis) and -0.02% ± 0.25% (head-neck/brain); Gamma index passing rates: 99.9% (pelvis), 99.8% (head-neck), 99.8% (brain) in high dose evaluation; 99.7% (pelvis), 99.5% (head-neck), 99.9% (brain) in medium dose evaluation. Auto-contouring - Dice score: 0.95 ± 0.03 (bladder), 0.90 ± 0.04 (colon), 0.96 ± 0.01 (femoral head); 95% Hausdorff distance: 2.69 ± 1.82 mm (bladder), 4.96 ± 3.91 mm (colon), 2.04 ± 0.49 mm (femoral head)
